journal_entry.reason: Dividend declared creates a liability (Dividends Payable) and increases the Dividends account, which reduces retained earnings

output.asset_increase_reason: No assets increase. Cash will decrease when paid in 30 days, but declaration does not affect assets yet.
output.dividend_increase_reason: Dividends account increases by $500,000 when declared. This is the debit side of the declaration entry.
output.expense_increase_reason: Dividends are distributions to owners, not expenses. They are classified as dividend increases, not expense increases.
output.liability_decrease_reason: No liabilities decrease. Declaration creates a liability (Dividends Payable), it does not decrease one.
output.equity_decrease_reason: Equity decreases occur on the credit side when dividends are declared. The debit side records the dividend account increase, not direct equity decrease.
output.revenue_decrease_reason: No revenues are affected by dividend declaration.

output.liability_increase_reason: Dividends Payable increases by $500,000 because the dividend has been declared but not yet paid, creating a liability to shareholders
output.equity_increase_reason: No equity increases; the declaration reduces retained earnings (equity), it does not increase it
output.revenue_increase_reason: No revenue is involved in dividend declarations; this is a distribution to owners, not an operating activity
output.asset_decrease_reason: No asset decrease yet; cash will decrease when the dividend is paid in 30 days, but at declaration only the liability is recorded
output.dividend_decrease_reason: No dividend account decreases; the dividend account increases on the debit side when declared
output.expense_decrease_reason: No expenses are involved; dividends are distributions of earnings, not expenses

output_tax_specialist.reasoning: Dividend declaration is not a taxable supply. No tax is mentioned in the transaction text. Dividends are distributions of equity to shareholders, not purchases or sales of goods/services.
